description: Show this command catalog. Add a word to filter, like groundwork-help git or groundwork-help update.
Fuzzy-pick a command from the catalog with fzf; prints it and copies it to the clipboard when possible.
Pull and apply Groundwork, resume in the freshly applied runner, then visibly update Homebrew, mise-managed runtimes, and latest-track AI tools.

Copy the last completed command and its output into a tmux buffer (and the clipboard when supported); a platform + directory line is prepended so pasted output tells an agent where it ran. Review for secrets before pasting into an AI tool.
Zoom the active pane to fill the screen; press again to restore. The status line shows Z while zoomed — this is what happened if a pane suddenly took over the screen.
Repo picker popup: fuzzy-search every repository discovered under your configured roots, preview its git status, and jump to (or open) its lazygit window.
Open a lazygit window for every repository with uncommitted changes (groundwork-repos changed).
Close the active pane. It asks first, because x sits next to z (zoom).
Preview dotfile changes before applying them to your home directory.
Apply the source repo to your home directory.
Git pull the Groundwork repo and apply it; syncs config, not already-installed tool upgrades.
Edit a managed dotfile at its source instead of editing the generated home file.
Open a shell in the Groundwork source repo.
Restore the first-run backup of managed files and macOS defaults.

Read-only machine health report. Checks the shell runtime (managed zsh installed, registered, adopted, running here), runtime release age, and Docker: daemon reachability, log rotation, image store, disk usage, and leftover containers/images/volumes with owner-scoped cleanup guidance.
Run only the Docker section of the health report.
Run only the shell runtime section: managed zsh installed, registered, selected as login shell, and running here.
Flag any mise-managed runtime adopted inside the 5-day release floor. A cooldown filters what you acquire; it never rolls back what you already have.

Dry-run Docker tidy, by enumeration: every image labeled dev.roost.ephemeral=true is inspected and reported as eligible (past the 72h grace by its strict-parsed dev.roost.built label, every tag in the scratch namespace, no container references), retained (in grace), protected (a non-scratch tag or a container reference), or unverified (missing jq or missing/malformed build label — retained, never removed); exited containers carrying the label are judged by the same grace window against their CreatedAt. Never volumes, never unlabeled images, never dangling images or builder cache.

Remove the eligible set: exited containers labeled dev.roost.ephemeral=true whose CreatedAt is past the same 72h grace (status re-checked immediately before removal), then each eligible image — re-checked immediately before deletion and deleted by explicit image ID. Container state under an ephemeral image is disposable only after the grace. Weekly, or before a large build, is plenty; update-all prints a one-line nudge when tidy-eligible leftovers pile up.

Dry-run preview of the DAEMON-WIDE cleanup: stopped containers, dangling images, and builder cache from every project on this machine, not just Groundwork. Owner-run only — update-all and scheduled agent paths never invoke it.

Apply the daemon-wide cleanup: prune stopped containers and dangling images, and prune builder cache toward the 5GB retention target (--keep-storage asks Docker to retain a target; it is not a guarantee). Never volumes, never tagged images.

Build a disposable Docker image under the ephemeral-build contract: the wrapper applies dev.roost.ephemeral=true, generates the RFC 3339 dev.roost.built timestamp itself, tags <project>/scratch:<purpose>, and prints the image ID. Add --rm-after to prove-and-delete; allowlisted docker build args go after -- (--file/-f, --target, --platform, --build-arg, --secret, --ssh, --no-cache, --pull, --progress; --output/--push are refused — the contract requires a local inspectable image).

Make Groundwork's Homebrew zsh your login shell (records your current shell first, registers the new one in /etc/shells, runs chsh, verifies the account record). Your previous shell stays untouched as the recovery path.
Report the shell runtime state (managed zsh, account login shell, $SHELL, recorded previous shell) and change nothing.
Restore the login shell you had before adopting. Works even when Homebrew is unavailable.
Restore a specific login shell, for when no previous one was recorded.

Table of every Git repository discovered under your configured roots (~/.config/groundwork/repos.conf): branch, dirty/clean as symbol + word, ahead/behind, worktree marker, path. Discovery is dynamic — a fresh clone appears within the cache TTL (30s), or immediately with --refresh, no config edit.

Fuzzy-pick a discovered repository (fzf) with a live git-status preview, then open or reuse its lazygit tmux window. Inside tmux it becomes a window; outside, it creates or attaches the repos session.
Open one lazygit tmux window per repository with uncommitted work — the fastest way to see everything your agents left unreviewed.
Scaffold AGENTS.md, agent folders, editor settings, and .gitignore for a new repo.
Scaffold a private agent-maintained knowledge wiki with raw/wiki layers and Obsidian setup.
Copy selected files or folders to the clipboard with path headers and contents for AI review.
Install the Groundwork-managed Obsidian plugin set into a vault.
Sign in to GitHub for gh and HTTPS Git operations.
Install gh as Git's HTTPS credential helper.
List files with details, hidden files, Git status, and directories first.
Tree view of the current folder, two levels deep; the quick shape-of-a-repo view.
Tree view, three levels deep, when lt is not enough.
Open lazygit.
Open Neovim.
Use bat for syntax-highlighted file viewing.
Jump to a frecent folder with zoxide.
Fuzzy-pick a folder, preview it as a tree, and cd into it.
Fuzzy-pick files with a bat preview and insert the chosen paths into the current command.
Open Yazi, a visual file browser with inline preview; navigate a folder, then quit to land the shell in whatever directory you were browsing.
Show the 50 largest files and folders under a path, followed by safe cleanup guidance for each result type.
Show only the largest files under a path; use / for the startup filesystem.
Show only the largest folders under a path; add -n N to change the result count.
Create a folder, including parent folders if needed.
Print the current folder path.
Open the current folder in Finder.
Search file contents quickly. Lowercase searches are case-insensitive by default.
Search hidden and gitignored files too, while still skipping .git internals.
Search with ripgrep, fuzzy-pick a result, preview it with bat, and open it in the editor.
Find files by name quickly.
Search shell history with atuin/fzf.
Run Atuin's opt-in English-to-shell assistant after setup.
List or set the default browser. Run defaultbrowser zen after setup.
List or open the recommended Chrome/Dia/Zen extension links.
List or open the recommended Raycast Store extension links.
BetterDisplay command-line interface for display automation.
Open a macOS app from the shell.
Pin a runtime/tool version in the current project.
Upgrade globally managed mise tools, including floating baselines such as Node LTS. Takes effect at your next prompt, even in already-open terminals.
Package manager installed through mise for JavaScript projects.
Python installed and versioned by mise; avoid relying on macOS system Python.
Fast Python package, project, script, tool, and virtualenv manager.
Show changed files.
Show unstaged changes.
Interactively stage parts of a change.
Commit staged changes.
Show the latest commit summary.
Start a named tmux session.
Attach to an existing tmux session.
Start a project layout from a checked-in tmuxp config.
Capture the current tmux session as a starter layout config.
Start tmux without restoring the previous resurrect snapshot.
Update tmux plugins from a normal shell when TPM's tmux pane is hard to read.
Save a tmux-resurrect snapshot now.
Restore the last tmux-resurrect snapshot.
Update tmux plugins with TPM; press Esc or Enter if the update pane does not repaint.
Install or repair tmux plugins with TPM.
Reload tmux config.
Rename the active pane.
Rename the active window.
Close the current window and all panes inside it.
End a named tmux session.
End every live tmux session; snapshots are separate.
Move between tmux panes and Neovim splits.
